response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, teaching ability, and some distributed systems experience, there are critical gaps in minimum requirements. Most significantly, the resume shows only 1-2 years of C++ experience from over a decade ago (last use 2015), far short of the required 8 years, with all recent work in TypeScript, JavaScript, and D. Infrastructure experience is limited to one 2-year data processing microservices role, not the required 5 years building large-scale cloud compute platforms. No formal technical leadership or mentoring role in cloud/infrastructure engineering is demonstrated.
strengths: Proven ability to architect and operate large-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests per second, with microservices and real-time data processing experience
Strong mentoring and teaching background (workshops, courses, open-source projects) demonstrating ability to establish best practices and share technical knowledge
Consistent track record of shipping products and mastering multiple languages/platforms, showing adaptability and technical depth
concerns: Only 1-2 years of C++ experience from 10+ years ago with no recent C++ work; current 10-year career is entirely TypeScript, JavaScript, and D—missing 8-year C++ requirement
Infrastructure experience limited to 2 years in data processing microservices (Dunnhumby); lacks the 5-year requirement for building cloud compute platforms or large-scale infrastructure
No demonstrated technical leadership role mentoring cloud or infrastructure engineers; recent staff role is only 2 months old with no track record of platform ownership

key_factors.factor: Distributed Systems and Software Craftsmanship Background
